candidate: L13 DAILY-PEAD TWO-GATE FEASIBILITY BAR (SYNTHESIS/feasibility). NO new edge, NO new data acquisition, NO optimization, NO re-test. Reads the FROZEN L8 (power band) + L9 (empirical arrival rate) + L10 (monthly liquid SUE effect + event sd) results and the committed D-208 realistic-cost artifact to fold the COST wall and the POWER wall into ONE forward bar for the decisive daily-PEAD experiment. Computes the MINIMUM gross announcement-window CAR a daily-PEAD leg must deliver to clear BOTH gates (net |t|=2 after realistic round-trip cost) as a function of holding window and horizon, decomposes it into cost-component vs power-component, identifies which wall binds at each horizon, and contrasts the resulting bar against the measured monthly liquid SUE signal. Upgrades FORWARD_DECISION_CARD from point-estimates to a two-gate bar.
purpose: L8 established daily-PEAD is the ONLY power-reachable event class IF its standardized effect lands in the measured liquid-event band; L9 grounded the arrival rate (~95-136/yr); L10 measured the monthly liquid SUE half-split (+0.69%, insignificant) and the event sd (~18.5%/mo) and framed daily-PEAD as a magnitude/recovery question. NONE of L8-L11 folded the D-207/D-208 realistic COST wall into the daily-PEAD reachability bar -- they reported significance (power) only. L13 closes that: it combines the two walls (cost + power) into a single forward bar for daily-PEAD, so the decision card states the EXACT gross window-drift the unmeasurable announcement-window effect must clear to be DEPLOYABLE (not merely significant), and which wall binds at each horizon. The bar uses ONLY frozen, measured inputs and the standard random-walk variance scaling; it does NOT put a prior on the unmeasurable daily-window effect -- it bounds the REQUIREMENT, not the effect.
inputs_FROZEN: lab-demo-goal/results/l10_pead_effect_results.json (LIQUID half-split effect_pos_minus_neg=+0.006944; mean_pos=+0.00377 [long-only high-SUE market-relative]; sd_event_liquid=0.185073)

method_FROZEN.window_sd_scaling: sigma_window = sigma_month * sqrt(H_hold / 21), the standard random-walk variance-time scaling (21 trading days/month). This is exactly the scaling L10's window_noise_caveat invokes; it is NOT a free parameter.
method_FROZEN.power_component: min window-CAR for |t|=2 with zero cost = T_SIG * sigma_window / sqrt(n), where n = arrival_rate * horizon_years.
method_FROZEN.cost_component: fixed per-event round-trip cost from D-208 liquid selected-picks (c_rt=0.003804). Long-only leg = 1 round-trip; long-short SUE-spread leg = 2 round-trips (BIST short constraints flagged).
method_FROZEN.two_gate_bar: min gross per-event window-CAR for NET |t|=2 = cost_component + power_component (cost shifts the whole power bar UP by the fixed cost). Cost FLOOR (n->inf) = cost_component: even with infinite data the gross drift must exceed the round-trip cost to be net-positive.
method_FROZEN.binding_wall: COST if cost_component >= power_component else POWER, per (leg, holding-window, horizon).
method_FROZEN.concentration_requirement: concentration_ratio = two_gate_bar / monthly_leg_effect = how many times the FULL monthly liquid SUE spread the few-day announcement window must deliver to clear the bar. <=1 means the full monthly spread (if perfectly concentrated) suffices; >1 means the window must deliver MORE than the entire monthly spread (not achievable from this signal without amplification).
method_FROZEN.cost_floor_vs_monthly: monthly_leg_effect / cost_component: if <1, the monthly leg effect does not even cover the round-trip cost -- net-positive is impossible from this signal at monthly resolution regardless of sample size; only announcement-window concentration above the cost floor can rescue it.
method_FROZEN.legs: LONG-ONLY high-SUE market-relative (effect=mean_pos, 1 round-trip) and LONG-SHORT SUE half-split (effect=effect_pos_minus_neg, 2 round-trips).

honest_expectation_DECLARED_BEFORE_RESUL.summary: Expect the two-gate bar to be DEMANDING and the binding wall to be POWER at short horizons (sigma_window/sqrt(n) large) shading to the COST FLOOR at long horizons (power-component shrinks with sqrt(n); the fixed round-trip cost remains). Expect the starkest finding to be that the measured monthly liquid SUE signal has LITTLE-TO-NO margin over the cost floor: long-only high-SUE monthly market-relative mean (+0.38%) is near/below a single round-trip (~0.38%), and the long-short half-split (+0.69%) is near a double round-trip (~0.76%). If so, daily-PEAD viability hinges ENTIRELY on whether announcement-window CONCENTRATION lifts the few-day CAR materially above the monthly average AND the true window noise is at/below sqrt-scaling -- BOTH unmeasurable offline. This would TEMPER (not kill) the #1 ranking: still the only power-reachable class, but the net-deployable bar is high and the monthly signal does not have margin to spare, so the fetch could well return NULL. HONEST caveats: (a) L10's window_noise_caveat says true window noise may be BELOW sqrt-scaling (post-announcement drift steadier than random walk), which would LOWER the bar; (b) the monthly SUE half-split is itself insignificant (may be noise); (c) canonical PEAD in developed markets DOES concentrate post-announcement, but that is unmeasured in BIST liquid names. Result recorded whatever it is.
honest_expectation_DECLARED_BEFORE_RESUL.most_likely_verdict: DESCRIPTIVE-FEASIBILITY-VIEW. A data-grounded two-gate bar that sharpens (not decides) the daily-PEAD go/no-go: viability hinges on unmeasurable announcement-window concentration beating a quantified cost floor. NO edge claim; result recorded whatever it is.
